title: agrostis exarata var. rossiae
intro: Agrostis exarata var. rossiae, also known as Ross's bentgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to have various medicinal benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The health benefits of Agrostis exarata var. rossiae include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Agrostis exarata var. rossiae is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and has also been used in traditional folk remedies, such as poultices and salves.
